# CamelRatiosIndex

## Overview

**CamelRatiosIndex** implements the multivariate-weighted indexing
method for bank performance assessment using the CAMEL framework. The
package computes composite year-on-year indices that enable:

- Comparison across multiple banks
- Assessment of bank health relative to a base year
- Evaluation of the overall banking industry health

Based on the methodology proposed by Ayimah et al. (2023a, 2023b). This
composite index is intended to offer regulators and policymakers a
standardised, objective for monitoring bank performance over time and
across institutions. Its ability to benchmark banks against a common
base year enhances early-warning capabilities, enabling supervisory
authorities to identify emerging weaknesses individual banks as well as
systemic vulnerabilities within the industry.

## Installation

``` r

# Install from GitHub (development version)
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("JC-Ayimah/CamelRatiosIndex")
```

## Quick Start

``` r

library(CamelRatiosIndex)

# inspect example datasets
head(camel_2015)   # used as base year's data
head(camel_2022)   # used as current year's data

# Compute CAMEL index
result <- camel_index(camel_2015, camel_2022)

# View results
result$index_table
#> # A tibble: 21 x 3
#>    bank      I_mw    PD
#>    <chr>    <dbl> <dbl>
#>  1 Absa     102.5  2.52
#>  2 AB        98.3 -1.72
#>  3 ADB      101.8  1.78
#>  ...

# Visualize
plot_camel_index(result, highlight_banks = c("Absa", "Ecobank", "GCB"))
```

## Features

- **Tidyverse-native**: Built on dplyr, ggplot2, and tibble
- **Flexible input**: Accepts data frames or matrices with bank names
- **Robust statistics**: Uses OGK robust covariance estimation via
  `robustfa`
- **Rich output**: Returns index table, weights, eigenvalues, and factor
  analysis objects
- **Publication-ready plots**: ggplot2-based visualization with
  customizable themes
- **Built-in data**: Example datasets from 21 Ghanaian commercial banks

## The CAMEL Framework

| Dimension                 | Description | Direction                      |
|---------------------------|-------------|--------------------------------|
| **C**apital Adequacy      | Ca          | Higher = better                |
| **A**sset Quality         | Aq          | Higher = worse (auto-inverted) |
| **M**anagement Efficiency | Me          | Higher = worse (auto-inverted) |
| **E**arnings              | Eq          | Higher = better                |
| **L**iquidity             | Lm          | Higher = worse (auto-inverted) |

## Functions

| Function | Description |
|----|----|
| [`camel_index()`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_index.md) | Compute composite CAMEL index |
| [`plot_camel_index()`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/plot_camel_index.md) | Plot percentage differences across banks |
| [`print.camel_index()`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/print.camel_index.md) | Print method for index results |
| [`summary.camel_index()`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/summary.camel_index.md) | Detailed summary of factor analysis |
| [`autoplot.camel_index()`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/plot_camel_index.md) | ggplot2 autoplot method |

for guidelines.

details.

## References

Ayimah, J. C., et al. (2023a). A Robust Multivariate Weighting Technique
for Computing a Measure for Inflation. *African Journal of Technical
Education and Management*, 3(1), 1-15.

Ayimah, J.C. (2023b). Computing Multivariate-Weighted Consumer Price
Index: An Application Manual in R. B P International.

# Package index

## Core Functions

Main package functions for CAMEL index computation

- [`camel_index()`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_index.md)
  : Compute Multivariate-Weighted CAMEL Index
- [`plot_camel_index()`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/plot_camel_index.md)
  [`autoplot(`*`<camel_index>`*`)`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/plot_camel_index.md)
  : Plot CAMEL Index Percentage Differences

## S3 Methods

Methods for camel_index objects

- [`print(`*`<camel_index>`*`)`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/print.camel_index.md)
  : Print Method for camel_index Objects
- [`summary(`*`<camel_index>`*`)`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/summary.camel_index.md)
  : Summary Method for camel_index Objects
- [`plot_camel_index()`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/plot_camel_index.md)
  [`autoplot(`*`<camel_index>`*`)`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/plot_camel_index.md)
  : Plot CAMEL Index Percentage Differences

## Datasets

Example data from Ghanaian banks

- [`camel_2015`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_2015.md)
  : CAMEL Ratio Data for Ghanaian Banks (2015)
- [`camel_2016`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_2016.md)
  : CAMEL Ratio Data for Ghanaian Banks (2016)
- [`camel_2017`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_2017.md)
  : CAMEL Ratio Data for Ghanaian Banks (2017)
- [`camel_2018`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_2018.md)
  : CAMEL Ratio Data for Ghanaian Banks (2018)
- [`camel_2019`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_2019.md)
  : CAMEL Ratio Data for Ghanaian Banks (2019)
- [`camel_2020`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_2020.md)
  : CAMEL Ratio Data for Ghanaian Banks (2020)
- [`camel_2021`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_2021.md)
  : CAMEL Ratio Data for Ghanaian Banks (2021)
- [`camel_2022`](https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/CamelRatiosIndex/reference/camel_2022.md)
  : CAMEL Ratio Data for Ghanaian Banks (2022)
